notes: Pre-containerisation baseline. GB = Great Britain. Industry = 'Port and inland water transport'; occupation = 'Stevedore and dock labourer'.

source_row: El-Sahli & Upward 2015 Fig 1 (Digest of Port Statistics 1968)
notes: 1967 stevedore figure is average for first 37 weeks of 1967 and excludes stevedores hired by ports not covered by the Dock Labour Scheme. First UK container ships docked 1966.
Tilbury container-ship ban (Jan 1968–Apr 1970) just ended. ~2/3 of 1961 stevedores remain.
London Docks final closures 1983. Industry employment for 1961–1981 is 'Port and inland water transport'.
NDLS abolished 1989; 7,200 registered dockers declared redundant 1989–1992 (Turnbull 1992). Stevedores down >90% from 1961 peak.
Industry figure for 2001 is 'Water transport' only (narrower classification than 1961–1981). Occupation still 'Stevedore and dock labourer'.
Industry figure not available for 2011. Stevedore count stable at ~5k floor.
